data.description:
# Prompt caching at the shared runner seam (CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*, provider-safe)

## Why
Every plan-review LLM call re-sends a large byte-identical prefix (system prompt + whole parent plan + tool
defs). pydantic-ai 1.107.0 prompt caching is provider-safe (no-ops off-Anthropic); caching this prefix is
FIDELITY-NEUTRAL (identical inputs/outputs) and the biggest single token win. Confirmed by OSS precedent
(Arize/phoenix, vitali87/code-graph-rag, shotgun-sh, josca42/varro).

## What
- Add a cacheable-prefix boundary to `RunRequest` (split stable prefix from volatile suffix; runner contract).
- `runner.py` (PydanticAIRunner.run): insert `pydantic_ai.CachePoint` into the USER-message content list
  `[stable_prefix, CachePoint(ttl="5m"), volatile_suffix]`; never make CachePoint the FIRST content block.
- Enable `anthropic_cache_instructions` + `anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` via `AnthropicModelSettings`,
  GATED to anthropic providers (`resolved.startswith("anthropic")`) — they error on openai/gemini. CachePoint
  stays portable (silently filtered off-Anthropic).
- Populate the prefix in `passes.py` (Pass-1 + container RunRequests): parent plan + roster = stable prefix;
  criterion/child = volatile suffix.
- Keep ≤3 of the 4 cache breakpoints (instructions + tool_definitions + CachePoint).
- Instrument cache_read/write tokens (extend runner observability) and surface per run.

## Scope
- In: RunRequest cache field; runner.py CachePoint + anthropic_cache_* gating; passes.py prefix wiring;
  cache-token instrumentation; prefix byte-stability.
- Out: engine RunnerAgentStep caching (S2); parallelization/warm-up (S3); merge (S4); bin-pack (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] runner.py inserts a CachePoint after the stable prefix; `anthropic_cache_instructions`/`tool_definitions`
      are enabled and gated to anthropic providers.
- [ ] On a repeat anthropic call sharing the prefix, `usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0`; on openai/gemini the
      call succeeds unchanged (provider-safe; no cache_* sent).
- [ ] The cached prefix is byte-stable — a stability test asserts no per-call interpolation
      (dates/UUIDs/unsorted JSON/variable tool order); cache reads stay non-zero.
- [ ] At most 3 cache breakpoints are used; no 4-breakpoint 400 error.
- [ ] cache_read/write token counts are surfaced per run (observability).
- [ ] No behavior/finding change (fidelity-neutral); `make lint` + tests pass.
data.title: Prompt caching at the shared runner seam (CachePoint + anthropic_cache_*, provider-safe)
